reviews.review_text:
This is one of the rare games I struggled to write a review for. I was so close to giving it a negative review, but then I thought that would be unfair to the developers, so I changed it to positive.

The biggest reason for this is that I have stayed away from the roguelike genre for years, and I forced myself several times to try it and see whether I could actually enjoy it or not. I was always prejudiced against the genre because I hate soulslike games, and because of that, I thought the whole “be born, progress, die, repeat” idea could annoy me just as much as soulslikes and ruin my enjoyment. Despite that, I bought a few games in this genre, like Hades, Dead Cells, Returnal, etc., and Hades was the first one I decided to properly try.

Well, turns out I was half right.

I can honestly say that I got more frustrated with every death, especially during the final boss fights against Hades near the end of the True Ending route, around the 8th-10th clears. Those fights almost made me break my keyboard. Some people will probably call this a skill issue, and honestly, fair enough, I respect that. But for me, the issue is more about not being a calm and patient person, which is also the same reason why I hate soulslike games.

The reason I said I was half right is that, even though there were moments that seriously annoyed me, I also enjoyed every bit of character progression. I could feel myself getting stronger, and because of that, most runs felt easier compared to the previous ones. I also thought the story was really good. I assume not every roguelike game puts much focus on story, but Hades definitely does not ignore that part. The amount of artifacts and things to grind also increases the replayability of the game. Even though that personally does not mean much to me, I am sure it is important for people who love this genre.

I finished the game with the True Ending after 10 clears, in almost 20 hours. After doing some research, I found out that even though I saw the credits, there is still an epilogue-related story if I keep playing. Honestly, I do not want to continue anymore because I got really bored. This boredom actually started after the 3rd clear, but instead of quitting, I wanted to keep going as far as I could to give the genre a proper chance.

There were many things I liked but I do not think the general structure of roguelike games is for me. Still, I will try the other roguelike games in my library and see how far I can go with them, but I do not think I will buy any more roguelike games from now on. I have respect for people who love the genre, but it is just not for me.

[b]To sum it up, there are 3 reasons why I am giving this game a positive review:[/b]
- It showed me that roguelike games are not for me.

- Even though I personally did not fully enjoy it, I still think it is a very good game within its own genre, and I do not want to be unfair to the developers.

- Dusa. Do not even ask for an explanation.

You know what I love about this game?
Everyone in Hell thinks they can stop you.

They talk like they understand power. Gods giving advice. Shades whispering warnings. Hades himself standing there acting like authority alone should be enough to keep you in line.

And every single run proves otherwise.

You fight your way out through rooms packed with monsters, traps, screaming souls, rivers of blood, all of it trying to slow you down. But the longer you play, the less threatening it becomes. Enemies stop feeling dangerous. They start feeling procedural. Obstacles arranged for your entertainment.

That’s when the game really clicks.

Combat feels incredible once you stop hesitating. Dashing through attacks, carving through entire rooms before enemies even finish reacting, stacking divine powers until the screen looks like the gods themselves lost control of the situation. It’s chaos for everyone else.

For you, it’s rhythm.

And I love the way death works here. You fail, crawl back home covered in ash and blood, and nobody’s surprised. They expect you to try again. They expect you to keep coming back.

What they don’t understand is that every death makes you calmer.

Sharper.

More certain.

Eventually the Underworld stops feeling like a prison and starts feeling like a machine designed to polish you into something unstoppable. Every boss, every weapon, every run feeding the same realization.

You were never trapped down there with them.
They were trapped down there with you.

By the end, escaping Hell barely even feels difficult anymore.
It feels natural. Inevitable.

Beautiful violence. Absolute confidence.
A game where even death eventually gets tired of standing in your way.

I wanted to platinum this game before reviewing it, and It took me a very, very long time to finally do it. This is an amazing video game, full stop. Roguelikes and roguelites are an oversaturated genre filled with stupid pixel games and vapid upgrade systems that offer basic stat boost or other ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥. This game id simply different. Not only is the gameplay fire, with plenty of weapons, build variety, and ways to change the gameplay up run to run, this game offers something 9 out of 10 roguelikes don't have at all; a story. a good one at that. with engaging characters, fantastic designs, aesthetics, and music, and lovely voice acting, this game offers it all. This is forever in the pantheon of top roguelikes ever, and while it doesn't offer the amount of content a game like Issac or risk of rain 2 does, it offers more heart and soul than 98 percent of games on the market. You would be remiss to skip this one.
What can I even say about this game that hasn’t already been said a million times? Hades is absolutely amazing. Addicting gameplay, incredible characters, and pure style from start to finish.

Great game - combat, visuals and soundtrack but its annoying that the story is drip-fed to you. You need to complete 10 successful runs to finish the main story, though being asked to play more isnt really a bad thing. Also many more playthroughs are needed to reach the epilogue, the criteria of which isn't made clear...

I've only got about 6 hours in, from what I've read im not even halfway through the "tutorial" phase of the game.  The art, the sound, the combat, I love pretty much every element of the game,  however for me personally I don't think I'll be able to continue playing much.   I'm really interested in the story however the gameplay loop revolves around escaping the underworld,  every death you return back to step one, you power up, you build relationships and take on new boons,  however I just don't really enjoy having to constantly return and start from step 1 every 30minutes.

Now if you're into that kind of thing then I can see this being an easy 9/10-10/10 for you,  as I've said I absolutely love everything else about this game, but it's not quite my cup of tea in terms of repetitiveness, which Is why I still recommend the game, but will rate it 8/10.   Whilst Hades doesn't quite fulfill what I want, it has at least made me really appreciate the genre and made me dive into it and explore other titles with similar gameplay but with more continuous gameplay loops vs repetitive.

- I like how game remembers things and react to them on consequent run. All characters react to things you choose, die from, kill. So it actually feels like a continuous events rather than repetition of same ones.
- Visual style is awesome, very distinct.
- Character design and writing is spot on.
- Game loop is done very well. Safe zone -> narrative interactions -> fights.

An amazing game! Absolutely loved the replayability as well! Story is also very well written

One of the things I enjoyed the most was that an increase in difficulty DOES NOT just increase the HP/ATK of the enemies. There were actual effort in making the enemies have new skills/gimmicks that make the fights more fresh and enjoyable!

Too much area damage, beating the game tells you that you didn't actually beaten the game. The easy mod setting starts at 20% and adds 2% after each death. I burned out on this game and after 6 mouth of not touching it, beaten it with cheat engine just to see what ending it has. Don't see much point of grinding it

Remembering 10(?) upgrade shops 1 for each god is impossible task for me. Also remembering what each reward is quite difficult

also a aloooot of spam from enemies and from player

This game is sick. I love the graphics and the unique take on Greek mythology. I love all the gameplay so far and have played for hours on end and been entertained the entire time. Would highly recommend
The best art direction and visual effect game ever created. It just feel so good every time you play it
Love it!!! Completed all achievements, only have Hypnos locked heart left and a lot grinding for decorating...

I wish Steam had an option to give games stars or something instead the binary "Would you recommend ..." Yes / No. For all the positives, this game is way too long if you're interested in the story and getting proper closure (the Epilogue ending) takes forever so at some point is just not worth it. No wonder only 8% of Steam players have gotten this. The game is fun for a while but I miss the times when 30 hours of gameplay was a long game and provided a much deeper and more satisfying story with more meaningful and better written dialogues.

Now for the long version of this review. I only started playing this because it's a game based on Greek mythology and I wanted to see what it was about. Regarding the story and the dialogues: As long as the writers stuck to the Greek myths and played around the fact that they exist in multiple sometimes conflicting versions, they did a great job. The characterizations of the Olympian gods is to the point and the game does a good job introducing players to some famous figures and storylines in Greek mythology.  The best part are probably the dialogues surrounding Zagreus' relationship to Dionysus. However, the storylines where the game deviates from Greek myths, are very weak and do not contribute to the story, e.g. [spoiler] the "romance" (or whatever that was) between Zagreus and one of Furies was pointless, bland and it feels like its target audience are 14-year-olds [/spoiler]. The writers could have also done a way better job with the main quest and the relationship between Hades and Persephone which ultimately falls flat. 

One of the main problems is also that the real ending is hidden behind many further hours of additional grind (which I did not bother with any more), i.e. how the game should have ended with Demeter finally finding Persephone and negotiating that she spends three-four months in the Underworld and the rest on the surface, which is the mythological explanation for the seasons (this is not a spoiler but basic Greek mythology), finally letting the snow on the surface melt (I don't know if this is what the epilogue does, but it would make sense and it would be a better ending). Not being able to get to this point in a reasonable time (and without clarity on the necessary steps for the story to progress) takes away from the game and makes it feel unfinished. Ultimately, while the game is fun for a while, if you're interested in the story more than in the gameplay, the game remains unsatisfactory. Also getting side quests done takes forever because of the underlying systems and the nature of the game. Ultimately, this is not an action-adventure RPG. 

Edit: This is not to say that the stories portrayed in Hades are accurate in respect to Greek myths, The main plot certainly is not and that's okay. That doesn't make it a very good story though and the ending is a non-ending. I did some google-ing and apparently the Epilogue ending does not contribute to the story much if at all. 


Edit 2: After 10 or so more hours I got to the Epilogue ending. No, it does not contribute anything to the story and is completely skipable. With all of the above said, this is a very fun game, if you don't try playing it like an RPG and don't expect much from the story. The only reason I'm keeping the review negative, is that I was not satisfied with many of the dialogues and with the main plot. Other than that I'd give it 6/10 or 7/10 (and 11/10 for the visuals and the music).

Not recommended.

I can see the polish, but the core loop does nothing for me. Run through random rooms, mash through combat, die, return, upgrade, repeat. If this is the point, then I find it tedious. I like mastery loops when replaying makes the same authored level cleaner and more cinematic. Clearly not a bad game, but wildly overrated for me. Refunded.

Hades is one of those rare games that feels impossible to put down. Every run is fast, stylish, and exciting, with combat that stays smooth and satisfying from start to finish. The voice acting, music, and art style are all incredible, but what really makes the game special is how every death still feels rewarding because the story keeps moving forward.

The characters are memorable, the gameplay never gets boring, and the progression system makes you constantly want “just one more run.” Even people who normally don’t enjoy roguelike games often end up loving Hades because it balances challenge, fun, and storytelling so perfectly.

A true 10/10 game that deserves all the praise it gets.
Makes me want to larp: 10/10 characters are really ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ cool and the gameplay is good (bonus points for any ragers out there).

I'd say it's pretty good.
I like how the Roguelike part of the game is canonically explained and that every run feels like it "makes sense" in the story.

I can play the game normally on Windows using any gamepad. However, when playing on macOS, none of the gamepads are usable, including the Xbox controller recommended by the game. I also hope the game can support all gamepads on macOS, specifically the Fantech Shooter 3 gamepad. if mac can use any game pad i will recomend this to my firend

VERY fun game.

Action-based combat is very fast, but you always feel like you're in control and it's comfortable. The Supergiant aesthetics and the dynamic OST mixing Greek and metal add to the experience. 

The story is the cherry on top - in other more popular roguelikes it's usually boring or non-existent, but here it's fully developed with THOUSANDS of voice-acted scripts of dialogue, all with witty humour in them. And, for my fellow non-Anglosaxon natives, the translations are just as good.

When you finish the game (should take you about 40 hours), the game is expanded even further with many more weapons for your character. Most importantly, it is also expanded with the "Heat" difficulty system, which is one of the most creative I've seen in any videogame. You can tweak any difficulty aspect you like (harder boss patterns, less item choices, more enemy health, timed runs...) and you can make thousands of different combinations.

So yeah, even if Hades II is out now, you can perfectly play this one for a more budget option.
My favorite game of all time. You can easily spend hours on this game, and probably will!

Asked an AI for a lightweight isometric indie game, because AAA titles nowadays are way too demanding, poorly optimized, and the prices for PC components and laptops are literally skyrocketing. Best decision ever. 

Honestly, everything about this game is a masterpiece, the story, artwork, mechanics, music, and voice acting all have their own unique charm. Even after 50+ hours and 70+ escapes, the game still gives you a reason to keep going and experiment with different boon builds. Plus, dying never feels frustrating; it always feels rewarding.

The only downside? RIP your controller. Don’t u ever used yout favorite one because you will literally spam those buttons to death.

Currently grinding for 100% achievements, and I honestly can’t wait for Hades II!

Absolutely top notch game. I postponed playing it because I thought I wouldn’t enjoy it, on account of being repetitive. How very wrong I was.

I sucked in my first bunch of runs, and would always die in either late Tartarus or early Asphodel. Then, on my 10th run I somehow got incredibly lucky with my shield built, and went through all the way to the Hades bossfight. For a while, it seemed that I would defeat him easily- but I didn’t know about the second healthbar. In the end, I very nearly beat him, but died. I was so frustrated for the longest time, as I was so close and so proud of myself.

Next couple of runs, nowhere close. I thought I wouldn’t never get the same epic run again. But that’s exactly where Hades surprises you: there’s so much potential for really cool runs. When you think a particular mix of boons couldn’t get better, well, it does. 

Ended up slaying Hades around run 14, and played a further 70-ish hours after that.
Took only 64 hours to platinate this easy game, I recomand it for beginers and masochists
awesome game, artstyle is peak, combat feels good, theres a lot of stuff to do and it can get suprisingly hard, i will try to 100% it at some point but for now im still new
Great Lore, gameplay, skills, fun, hack and slash

Hades is a fantastic game. Just 100%'d the game and i can say that the 143 hours i've spent on this game was well worth the time. Fantastic story, art style, gameplay loop, everything. Cannot recommend a game more
Amazing game, always feels fresh, even with the same escape attempt loop
First rouguelike game, i was initially skeptical but ended up loving the gameplay.
great game - from storytelling to game play and the well thought through rogue aspect

This is one of my all time favorite games.  It's one of the main reasons I found I love roguelike games.  The gameplay is fluid.  The game is challenging without being brutally punishing like the Dark Souls, which are personally too punishing for me, and the runs are different enough to make replaying fun, not letting it get monotonous.  I actually only just got to the epilogue on one of my games and there's still things to unlock.  (my hours may be a little deceptive)

This game is very fun and runs very well on my ancient laptop with intergrated graphics. The only reason I give it an unrecommanded is that it's very hard to distingush between everything when it's moving and try to actually dodge the attacks. It's even worse if your screen is small, you have trouble with eyesights or you have attention problems. Having played Hades 2 it's clear how much the devs have improved on visibility problem so if you are afraid you have problem with visibility I highly recommand you jump straight to Hades 2.

I've just now finished trying a playthrough with every weapon type, so I guess I can leave a review. I thought I would enjoy this game a lot. I have played Diablo 3 at a pretty high level, for probably thousands of hours (so similar gameplay type), and I've beaten Rogue Legacy 1 and 2 (so, similar "rogue-lite"-ish loop), and also beaten Dead Cells on ng+1 and maybe +2. Specifically I have even done the huge grind in RL2 to do all of the secret postgame bosses, and have done the same on RL1.

But frankly I can only tolerate this game (barely) while I'm also watching a Youtube video in another monitor. It's so bloody boring to sit there and have to grind all the way up to where I was at previously EVERY SINGLE RUN. This game would benefit a lot from cutting repetition down by letting you start out in a zone you let off at previously. There's already going to be a penalty because you won't have any of the powerups gained earlier.

Specifically, the 3rd boss (which I'm getting to fairly consistently) is just such a massive step up from the previous ones, and apparently they will randomly throw alternate versions of the first two bosses at you as well. This ♥♥♥♥ just takes too long. It's boring. There's not enough variations in room or enemy types. Some enemies are just plain terrible if you don't have the right boons, and each run just takes way too long. 

The time vs enjoyment tradeoff is just not there for me. I can't even see myself trying to get better at this game, because it's not like the enemies have any fun or complex mechanics. Most of their attacks are pretty basic. I'm mostly just mindlessly grinding to each boss, where I might or might not get completely ♥♥♥♥♥♥ because I barely get to practice them at all (and even less the alt forms). So, I don't get the hype behind this. I might try playing more sometime, but at the moment I don't recommend it personally. Somehow not my thing despite the fact that in every way it should be.

Rogue Legacy 1/2 felt much better to play because I could just return and retry bosses when I failed, and thus I could feel myself getting better with every attempt, and every run of a biome. Here? Not really. I found myself getting more bored with every run. I REALLY... REALLY hate games where they just insert mechanics JUST to waste time like this so you'll play longer. RL1/2 let you cut out anything you didn't want, in exchange for some downsides. This game? Haven't seen anything. I guess you could just check "God mode" and then walk over to where you were previously before actually starting to play, but even this would still waste a lot of time.

There's is only about 12 hours of unique gameplay and only 1 fun weapon, yet the developers foolishly lock true completion behind the tedium of 10 successful runs. I guess I'll never know how Zagreus resolves his daddy issues...

‘Way down in hades way down under the ground’
I love this game so much, I’m not normally a rouge like player but this game is so addicting when you start you just want to finish but then you die 😔

Finally managed to escape the Underworld. While I've never particularly enjoyed the roguelike genre given the seemingly Sisyphian task given my skill issues, I really enjoyed the original storyline with the fresh interpretations of greek mythology. Can't believe how many new dialogues and stories this game still has even after the hours put in. Would probably attempt to clear more runs given time to unlock them all. 

Recommended even if you're new to the genre and especially so if you're interested in mythology.
Come for the rougelite hack and slash, stays for the disfunctional family drama.
This game was on my backlog for so long. I knew it was amazing and I finally got the time to get through it. Absolutely incredible from start to finish. I would recommend this for sure.

A really good game and highly addictive.  It's definitely a one more go kind of game.  That said I have started to play other games to break things up as after 100+ hours it does start to get repetitive, though I do intend to finish it.

Plays great on the Steam Deck.  I only played it through the TV.
Great game. Surprisingly many hours of gameplay, even after you "complete" the game. One of the best roguelike games with amazing range of difficulty, depending on how you customize your runs.
Wouldn't have finished grad school without having the option to start my day with a Hades run. It's like meditation, only faster and bloddier.

A dangerously addicting masterpiece that will get you addicted within 5 minutes of gameplay,with cool and unique artstyle,actually interesting story,great soundtracks and for those who, are like me and love to 100% complete the games they like, a wonderful completion run.
Well you're gonna have a lot of "runs" while exploring all of this game's mechanics,dialogue,and options but no two runs will ever be the same.Its weird really the whole concept of the game is reaching the surface form tartarus attempting to do it against the same bosses and mobs yet it somehow doesnt feel repetitive or cause burnout which is very interesting.
Definetely pick his up if its on offer but even if you pay full price you'll definetely get your money's worth back in content
10/10 masterpiece roguelite

[h1]One of the greatest games of all time.[/h1]
[h2]A visual masterpiece with a captivating artstyle.The character designs are [i]chef kiss[/i].[/h2]
[h3]It is amazing how the game range from comedic,heartwarming and heartbreaking.[/h3]

Hades,story based on ancient greek mythology,is a roguelike hack and slash game with narrative storytelling that keeps you hooked.The music really good and the voice acting is perfectly done for every characters.The games feel alive by responding to your scenarios,keeping you captivated.
[spoiler]The sunrise scene gets me everytime and the music,atmosphere that follows afterward is enough to make my heart move.[/spoiler]

[u]All in all,this is a game I would recommend to anyone without hesitation.[/u]

Great game, maybe first rogue-lite ever? but definitely in 5+ years. Wasn't sure I would like it but after getting a hang on the controls, story and how to progress, I pretty quickly put 40+ hours. Easily worth the $8 I paid and would still have been worth it at full price. Will be picking up Hades 2 once I move on from this.
Very fun and addictive. Nice story and great dialogues.
guy with daddy issues tries to run away from home and fails but its okay because everyone is hot for some reason

this was my first ever rouge like game, usually i only play single player story based games like halo and stuff but wow this was the perfect introduction for me to this genre, took a bit longer to finish (i don't do 100% just the story) but it was absolutely amazing.
because of this i am definately going to check other rouge-like games now
A gem of a game. Mechanics, graphics, stories...all of it so dialed in.

First rougelike game I've played. It is pretty fun and progressive. The average joe should be able to complete the main story after multiple attempts. The complete story and epilogue will require a lot more skill, effort and perserverance to keep replaying...
The gameplay loop is quite fun.  Each run is enjoyable as you work to upgrade yourself for the next one.
Its beautiful, its frustrating, its silly, its hard, then its easy, then its hard again because you make poor choices...

I bought this game for 7 bucks, I thinks is the best value to my money I’ll ever get. No doubt one of the greatest games of all time. 
Its biggest issue is that sometimes it does feel weird due to its 2.5D graphics but you’ll get used to it. 9/10 game 10/10 story (in the setting) 10/10 music and infernal/10 gameplay
I haven't beaten it yet so I'll update this when I beat it, but as of where I am right now it is an amazing and very cool game.

Die, die again.

Love the soundtrack and mythology.

Still playing the game and I never want to stop.

10/10 recommend
very solid game.
surprisingly good story.
very much fun.

Hades is hell of a game; it uses the video game medium to tell a story in a masterful way. Every run is different, even though there are ways to control certain aspects of each run you play. There are runs that, due to RNG, are going to be much easier than others, but that is the magic of a roguelite in this case.

One of the best things about it is the characters and how you interact with them. Although there are no dialogue choices, every interaction feels organic and encourages you to play so that you get invested in the story. It’s incredible that once you finish your first run and meet your mother, it makes you want to reach that point even more and learn the full story
fun interesting gimmick cool bosses. fun progressison

You might struggle at first but once you get the hang of it you'll definitely get hooked. This game offers a lot of variety and it's very much worth the money than some if not most AAA games out there. Supergiant Games is goated for this.

I am really Enjoying Hades. Endless replay ability. These games respects your time. Easy to pick up and get right into the action. Progress is saved at the start of each room. When I die in the game I'm always left feeling excited about the next run. Deaths never seem to feel frustrating or cheap. Often I find myself wanting to squeeze in one more run. 
Great feeling! Controls well. Combat and abilities are varied and deep. The story is touching and interesting, with a mystery goals that keep you going. Really nice art style and character designs. Great sound with fully voice characters and nearly endless conversations. I haven't had any repeated conversation in over 25 hours of my playtime. 100% Recommended!

10/10 games. chudmaxxed my way into 100%ing the game within about a week and a half. helped me reach low cortisol levels during finals for college and now during summer break. highly recommend to every chud and looksmaxxer alike.
roguelike so good it has spawned countless copycats that try to imitate it with different styled artwork or mythos, but it can't replace the best game of 2020.

Okay. This is one of the best games of all time.

The amount of polish and attention to detail poured into absolutely everything is astounding. It puts many AAA games to shame. Hades is proof that you don't need an unlimited budget, gigantic open worlds, or endless cinematic set pieces to create something fun. It is phenomenal.

The game barely puts a foot wrong. The story and voice acting are sublime from start to finish. Zagreus in particular is a fantastic protagonist, with a calm confidence that makes him immediately likeable. The amount of dialogue is absurd, and almost all of it is excellent. Characters always seem to have something new to say, even long after you have completed the main story, and those conversations constantly add depth to the world and overarching plot.

What really impressed me was how reactive everything feels. Characters remember your previous runs, comment on your actions, react to gifts, acknowledge new abilities, and even mock you for changing your mind later. I think it took around 70 hours before some conversations started feeling a little less fresh, and honestly, that is an absurd amount of time for dialogue to remain engaging. Oh and the few times the game tries to be comedic it lands perfectly.

The combat is equally impressive. At first it felt a little button mashy, but once I unlocked the second dash and started experimenting with different weapons, I realised just how much depth there is. There are six weapons in total, each with four aspects that often radically change how they play. Many of them feel like entirely different weapons. The game also cleverly incentivises you to rotate between them, keeping each run feeling fresh.

The boon system is fantastic too. As you play, you begin to understand which gods synergise best with your preferred weapons and playstyle, and how different boons can combine into incredibly powerful builds. The best part is that even when the RNG refuses to cooperate, the game almost always gives you enough tools to cobble together something fun and effective. Across roughly 70 runs, I can only remember one where I felt genuinely underpowered, and even that run was still enjoyable.

The bosses are excellent, and the game does a great job of keeping them relevant. Once you complete the main story, you can enhance them with new abilities and mechanics, preventing them from becoming repetitive. The minibosses are great too, for the most part. Except that one with the Medusa head and the stompy thing. I didn't have the patience for that one. 

Across roughly 80 hours, I never felt bored. If anything, I was repeatedly surprised by how well the game continued to evolve. The combat remained engaging, the story kept expanding, and the characters never stopped being interesting.

It is incredible.

Hades is genre defining. It sets a high bar for Hack and Slash Roguelikes. The art is incredible, it has astounding voice acting, and the story is a brilliant Greek myth inspired drama. There are dozens, hundreds, or if you really want even thousands of hours of gameplay. It's only $25. I can highly recommend this.
Absolute masterpiece. While tough, the gameplay is extremely addictive. I love the artstyle and the soundtrack is full of bangers. There's always a reason do do just one more run...

If you're a fan of roguelike games, **Hades** is an absolute must-play.
I've spent countless runs experimenting with different weapons, Heat levels, and boon combinations, and the game never gets old. Every run feels unique because of the incredible variety in builds you can create. Whether you're chasing a powerful duo boon, trying a new aspect, or pushing yourself with higher Heat, there's always something fresh to experience.
What really makes Hades stand out is how it blends fast-paced, satisfying combat with meaningful progression and an engaging story. Even when a run ends in failure, it never feels like wasted time because you're constantly unlocking new dialogue, upgrades, and strategies.
The weapon variety is fantastic, and each one plays differently enough to keep the gameplay exciting. Combined with the endless possibilities offered by the Olympian boons, no two runs ever feel exactly the same.
Whether you're new to roguelikes or a veteran looking for a game with incredible replayability, Hades delivers. It's one of those rare games where "just one more run" easily turns into hours of fun.
Highly recommended for anyone who enjoys roguelikes, build experimentation, and challenging but rewarding gameplay.
Without a single doubt in my mind, this game is the greatest game OF ALL TIME. simply put, a MASTERPIECE. No flaws, everything else is beyond excellent.
It is one of if not the best roguelite games that I have ever played, the story is top notch, and the mythology is amazing to 10/10
Incredible game. Truly incredible game. A million recommendations. Gorgeous artstyle, compelling characters, great humor. Love it.

Amazing game where dying over and over doesn't feel repetitive. Each run you get different boons and weapons to choose from, each with their own advantages and disadvantages, which makes each run feel fresh. The enemies aren't insanely difficult to beat, but also aren't too easy as to be boring. The story, characters, and art are also god-tier (literally and metaphorically). Highly recommend.
Put nearly all my hours I have in this game while I had Covid for the first time. This game is the best way to pass over a Covid sickness. One of the best rouglites out there.

Almost a 10/10 were it not for these flaws:

- when you restart (or in this game "Give up"), the world layout doesn't change. You have to die for that to happen, and that can take a while. This is just absolutely stupid. The whole point of roguelikes is to start fresh every time. 
- the dialogue becomes incredibly boring near the end of the game.

Genuinely the best roguelike I've ever played. The art is spectacular and the gameplay is so fun. I 100% recommend to anyone who has played or wants to get into roguelikes. Heck anyone in general. Probably one of my favorite games out there. 10/10

I may be awful at combat and unfortunately sending dear Zagreus back to Hades very often I am immensely enjoying my time playing the game!! The voice acting, the story, lore, atmosphere and artstyle are so engaging! I also appreciate the many assists that are there for us unskilled-combatants :)

Joined the Hades hype very late. Such a good game! Everything is amazing, the story, the art, the gameplay. You can spend several straight hours playing and learning dynamics, or play a quick run for 30 minutes just to have a little fun.
love dungeon crawlers!! getting good at all the different weapons is fulfilling, and the boon combinations are so fun! game so good that i feel like i am climbing out of there with zag.
Great game and I am looking forward to playing the second
Great gameplay loop, it's fun to build synergies and the story is immaculate. Overall a great experience with some fun achievements :)
Great game! A lot of dying and retrying so if that isn't your forte maybe give this a pass. But the story and concept is great. I like this a lot!
A fantastic game with so much content packed in for such a cheap price. Even when the game is about doing the same thing repeatedly, they still manage to make each attempt different.
A roguelike that I enjoyed repeating a lot and i mean A LOT, because I was invested in the story right from the start.

Fantastic game with incredible story, music, atmosphere, artstyle, writing, dialogue, and gameplay. 

If you are even remotely interested in roguelite games that allow you to get stronger after every run, pick this up. 9/10.
I don't need to tell anyone who's played this how awesome it is. I have some work to do learning the nuances of the game but it's definitely a blast to play and has a great story behind it.
I don't usually like rogue-likes but this is a lot of fun, and the God Mode mechanic means you don't necessarily need to 'get good' to enjoy the game.
lot of replay value. good storyline and mechanics

Hades is the game that made me understand why people love roguelites.

Fast combat, perfect pacing, incredible characters, amazing music, and a story that somehow gets better every time you die.

That is the magic trick: losing never feels like wasting time. Every failed run gives you progress, dialogue, upgrades, drama, family issues, and one more excuse to say “okay, last run.”

It is stylish, addictive, polished, and ridiculously well written.

10/10 — Greek mythology, daddy issues, and the cleanest roguelite loop ever made.
I'm not usually into roguelike games, but this game is something else man. The characters are great, and the gameplay is amazing.
Pirated this game, it was so good that I 100% completed it then I came here and bought it. Outstanding work

10/10, goated dating sim and fishing game

Jokes aside the combat, story, artstyle and music are all incredible and I highly recommend the game to anyone who hasn't played it yet. The story weaves in the roguelite aspects with the worldbuilding really well imo. Also, you can pet the dog. One of my favorite games ever.
One of my all time favorite titles, tons of replay value, easy to play, and overall just a fun game in the roguelite genre. 10 stars!

I really enjoy this game. It is my first time playing a... idk what its called, rogue-like? where you have to restart when u die, and I surprisingly really really enjoy it! Dying doesn't feel like a major setback, it's something that I can look forward to for the characters that reside in the house. The combat is also pretty easy to get used to in the beginning, but has a LOT of diversity and... well, abilities to choose from because of the boons. One more thing I love, THE DIALOGUE. ITS GENUINELY AMAZING. Zag's snarky remarks always make me laugh. Also the story is slightly bittersweet and I wont spoil anything but I really enjoyed it <3 (I havent finished the game myself yet but I watched people 100% it so yeah). Overall 10/10 amazing game. If you like rogue-likes and dont have this yet on your wishlist (cause summer sales are coming soon), idk what you're doing, you're missing out
Love Hades a lot. Beautiful game with amazing art, voice acting, combat and music. Supergaint games nailed it with this one. 10/10 just buy and play it.

Still one of the best roguelites out there! Great artstyle, enjoyable story and characters, high replayability and such a fun gameplay that doesn't bore or frustrate. You get to choose your own difficulty and go at your own pace. Highly recommended even with a sequel out.

One of the most overrated games I've played. Incredibly grindy, while I don't find the runs to have enough variation to merit this / maintain engagement. Some incredibly cringy voice acting - Zagreus, Thanatos, Meg.
Enjoyed the meta progression but not enough to continue.

I have over 100 hours on hades, and 130 hours on hades 2. It is easily one of the best games ever made. The story, the gameplay/mechanics, the characters, the amazing score, this game does not disapoint.

Bought it on a sale and jesus honestly the game is worth the whole price point , I'm like 20 hours in cleared the game a few times and there's still new interactions I'm discovering with the NPC's and storry on top of new mechanics I'm discovering , the game is absolutely packed with content and plays amazingly
Very good game, it got a bit samey closer to the end but regardless I really enjoyed the whole thing start to finish
first time I play just think for fun , not try hard for game but so long time I find 1 game so peak , perfect  game

I love this game. It's one of the games that initially got me into gaming back when I first got my Switch in college. Playing through it for 100% for the first time was both amazing and tedious. You can really tell that Supergiant figured out what worked and didn't work for when they made Hades 2. By itself, this game is a beautiful work of art with super fun gameplay and a bit of a grindy achievement system. Ultimately, I ended up pretty much having finished all the achievements apart from 3 by the time I was able to reach the epilogue. One of my favorite games.

I cannot believe that I did not get into this masterpiece of a game sooner. Music is really good, the controls are snappy, the weapons are fun even with the small on surface variety. The game is tons of fun and is difficult enough for even the seasoned gamer that I feel like I am. In the name of Hades, 9.5 out of 10 game.
Really fun story! Love the art and the music... and killing hordes of enemies :)
Guys I know I haven't played much so far but I fear I may be becoming addicted to this game oh my god..... absolute PEAK
